closed_reason: Docs + full verification matrix complete; epic checklist evidence captured
description:
**Problem**
Architecture refactors drift quickly without synchronized docs and verification evidence.
design: Update architecture/workflow docs and run full verification matrix including slow tests and boundary lint.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content:
Closeout evidence for bd-21eg.28:

Documentation updated for final crate split + boundaries:
- `docs/CRATE_DAG.md`
- `docs/architecture/boundary-api-migration-matrix.md`
- `docs/architecture/phase2-crate-split-closeout.md`

Boundary / epic acceptance proof points:
- No CLI->daemon direct imports remain (`rg -n "crate::daemon::" crates/beads-rs/src/cli crates/beads-cli/src` => no matches).
- `beads-cli` and `beads-daemon` compile in workspace flows.
- Boundary lint is active and passing (`just dylint`).
- Surface/API contract boundary remains typed and single-source through `beads-surface`/`beads-api` interfaces.

Full verification matrix run and passing:
- `cargo check`
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `just dylint`
- `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
- `RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 cargo test --features slow-tests`

Acceptance mapping:
- [x] Docs updated for final crate layout
- [x] Full verification commands pass
- [x] Epic acceptance checklist demonstrably satisfied

title: Documentation + full verification closeout for crate split

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Broadcast/hot‑cache can hold opaque bytes without type lies.
- [ ] Canonical bytes are only constructed via canonical encoder (or via an explicit `*_unchecked`).
- [ ] No `EventBytes<Canonical>` created from opaque remote bytes.
description:
**Problem**
`BroadcastEvent` requires `EventBytes<Canonical>`, but replication ingest accepts `EventBytes<Opaque>` and may forward non‑canonical CBOR bytes (allowed in v0.5). In `ingest_remote_batch` we wrap opaque bytes as canonical, so the type lies about the invariant.

**Signals / Evidence**
- `BroadcastEvent.bytes: EventBytes<Canonical>` (`src/daemon/broadcast.rs`).
- Remote ingest constructs `BroadcastEvent` by wrapping `EventBytes<Opaque>` into `EventBytes<Canonical>` (`src/daemon/core.rs`).
- `EventBytes<Canonical>::new` is public, so any caller can claim canonicality without validation.

**Why this hurts velocity**
This is a classic “types lying” case. It makes it impossible to reason about whether bytes are canonical and forces reviewers to audit every call site instead of trusting the type.
design:
**Design**
- Change `BroadcastEvent` to carry `EventBytes<Opaque>` (or add an enum/marker for `Canonical | Opaque`).
- Only label bytes as canonical when produced by the canonical encoder.
- Restrict `EventBytes<Canonical>::new` to `pub(crate)` and expose a constructor that actually enforces canonical encoding (or rename to `new_unchecked` with clear semantics).
- Update replication broadcast/hot‑cache to use opaque bytes and avoid implicit canonical claims.
title: BroadcastEvent should not require canonical bytes (type-state leak)

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All CLI Request constructors are converted to ctx/payload.
- [ ] CLI tests updated where they build Request directly.
description:
**Problem**
CLI command handlers construct `Request` using the old flat shape. After the Request refactor, these calls must be updated in a consistent, ergonomic way.

**Design**
- Add `mutation_ctx()`, `read_ctx()`, `repo_ctx()` helpers on CLI `Ctx` (as in REFACTORING_PLAN.md Phase 3).
- Update all CLI command files to construct `Request::{… { ctx, payload } }` using shared payload structs.
- Keep all fields and defaults identical to prior behavior (especially Claim default lease).
- Update any CLI unit tests that construct `Request` directly (search `Request::` in `crates/beads-rs/src/cli`).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] All CLI commands compile against new Request shape.
- [ ] No behavior changes in CLI options/flags.
- [ ] `cargo test -p beads-rs cli::mod` (and any affected CLI tests) passes.

**Files:**
- crates/beads-rs/src/cli/mod.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/cli/commands/*.rs
design: Use the new ctx/payload types; prefer small helpers to avoid repetition. Preserve existing flag → field mappings verbatim.
title: CLI: switch Request construction to ctx/payload + helpers

description:
**Problem**
beads-rs \`bd dep add <issueID> <dependsOnID> --kind <kind>\` uses \`--kind\`. Go \`bd dep add <issueID> <dependsOnID> --type <type>\` uses \`--type\`. Gascity calls the Go form (bdstore.go:760).

**Design**
Accept \`--type\` as an alias for \`--kind\` on the \`bd dep add\` subcommand. Keep both accepted — don't force a rename on existing beads-rs users.

clap supports multiple flag names via \`.alias(\"type\")\` or \`#[arg(long, visible_alias = \"type\")]\`. Use the visible-alias form so \`--help\` documents it.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] \`bd dep add a b --type blocks\` works
- [ ] \`bd dep add a b --kind blocks\` still works
- [ ] \`bd dep add --help\` mentions both
- [ ] Same alias considered on \`bd dep remove\` / rm if that command accepts a type flag too (check current signature)
- [ ] \`cargo xtest\` green

**Independent of**
All other Floor 0 work.

**Files likely touched**
- \`crates/beads-cli/src/commands/dep.rs\`
title: Floor 0 CLI: bd dep add accept --type as alias for --kind
